channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: The whole hill is dotted in pot holes and needs serious attention, 24th March
item.description:
Gates of Foxcote manor
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A436, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole - more than one on this road, 24th March
item.description:
It is hard to pinpoint a property on this road. It was dark, road is not well lit. Many potholes along this road. Some have been repaired, new ones have appeared. I wish to make a claim due to the damage caused to our vehicle.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckhampton Lane, Shurdington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass across pavement, 24th March
item.description:
There are lots of little shards of broken glass across the pavement here as you head down onto Hayden Road which have been there for a few weeks now.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayden Road, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Yellow lines have not been re-instated since tarmacing., 24th March
item.description:
Section of road from the corner of Pilley Lane and Leckhampton Road to the entrance of the new Coop shop car park requires Double Yellow lines re-instating and clear NO STOPPING signage. When John Wilkins garage was there double yellow lines were in place, since the new development and the pavement and road being ‘tartmaced’ the yellow lines have not been replaced. This is now more important due to the vast increase in pedestrians and vehicles.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 170-172, Leckhampton Road, Leckhampton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 24th March
item.description:

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 24th March
item.description:
Pothole CW 400x300x40mm approx Standard TM (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Footway adjacent to 50 Hatherley Road, Cheltenham, 24th March
item.description:
This is a re-report of FixMyStreet ref 8916399 (closed 19 Feb 2026 — "does not meet criteria for urgent repair"). We are formally disputing that assessment and requesting a re-inspection with measurement recorded. The defect is a significant level depression in the footway at the vehicle access point to No. 50. The original report noted: tripping incidents observed, vehicles grounding on the edge, standing water, and the issue worsening since at least 2020. Since that report, the resident and ward councillors have visited the site and gathered additional photographic evidence showing the vertical drop at the footway surface. We believe this meets or exceeds the 20mm intervention level for footway defects set out in GCC's Highway Safety Inspection Manual (August 2023). We are requesting: A re-inspection with the vertical deviation measured and recorded Confirmation in writing of the measurement taken and how it was assessed against the 20mm intervention threshold If the defect does not meet the intervention level, we would ask GCC to confirm this in writing so the resident can consider alternative routes (including a dropped kerb application) on an informed basis. This is a pedestrian safety issue — not solely a vehicle access issue. A tripping hazard at this level affects all footway users, including elderly residents and those with mobility impairments.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Hatherley Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 24th March
item.description:
Tree causing bird faeces and leaves on newly refurbished building
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked Gutter box in pavement ingress/flooding into basement, 24th March
item.description:
No 3 All Saints Road. We have had water ingress/flooding in the basement of this house. We have now had the gutter and down pipe cleaned out but water is still going down into the basement and fungus is now growing. Our contractor has been out to look at the black box gutter (pictured) but the section nearest the downpipe will remove the paving slab is moved and it is blocked causing water to keep running down the wall - this has been on going for 12 months we understand

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, All Saints' Road, Fairview, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old damaged cones, 24th March
item.description:
On the A436 between Pegglesworth and the Kilkenny Inn. Despite reporting this twice in the past (still showing on this website), there remains at least 16 old cones on the verges. Why can't these be tidied away?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gloucester Road, Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Signs & Barriers left behind by Contractor
